entries.title: Definition of a Limit
entries.content: The limit of f(x) as x approaches a is L if f(x) gets arbitrarily close to L as x approaches a (from either side). Foundation for derivatives and continuity.

entries.title: Power Rule (Derivative)
entries.content: Derivative of x raised to a constant power. Multiply by the exponent and reduce power by one. Applies to polynomials term by term.

entries.title: Product Rule
entries.content: Derivative of a product of two functions. Not the product of derivatives — use: first times derivative of second plus second times derivative of first.

entries.title: Fundamental Theorem of Calculus
entries.content: Links differentiation and integration. If F is an antiderivative of f on [a,b], then the definite integral of f equals F(b) - F(a).

entries.title: Chain Rule
entries.content: Derivative of a composite function. Differentiate the outer function while keeping inner unchanged, then multiply by derivative of inner function.
